name: Hanging Obelisk of Ramses II
description: A monumental red granite obelisk of Ramses II displayed in a unique suspended position, symbolizing royal authority and divine power.

name: Colossus of Ramses II
description: A colossal statue weighing over 80 tons, representing Ramses II in a majestic standing pose at the museum entrance.

name: Victory Column of Merneptah
description: A historical monument celebrating the military victories of King Merneptah, inscribed with significant achievements.

name: Statue of Senusret I
description: A refined statue representing Senusret I, reflecting the artistic precision of the Middle Kingdom.

name: Head of Amenhotep III
description: A sculpted royal head of Amenhotep III showing idealized features and fine craftsmanship.

name: Statue of Hatshepsut
description: A powerful depiction of Queen Hatshepsut in kingly form, emphasizing her authority as a female pharaoh.

name: Statue of Thutmose III
description: A statue of Thutmose III, one of Egypt’s greatest military rulers, symbolizing strength and expansion.

name: Seated Statue of Ramses II
description: A seated statue of Ramses II representing stability, divine rule, and timeless authority.

name: Statue of Seti II
description: A statue of Seti II reflecting the artistic traditions and royal continuity of the late New Kingdom.

name: Double Statue of Ramses II and Ra-Horakhty
description: A symbolic double statue showing Ramses II alongside the sun god Ra-Horakhty, emphasizing divine kingship.

name: Statue of Amenemhat III
description: A powerful statue of Amenemhat III highlighting the strength and realism of Middle Kingdom sculpture.

name: Statue of Nectanebo I
description: A statue of Nectanebo I representing the last native Egyptian dynasties before foreign rule.

name: Statue of Merneptah
description: A statue of King Merneptah emphasizing royal authority and continuity after Ramses II.

name: Statue of Emperor Augustus
description: A Roman-era statue of Emperor Augustus depicted in Egyptian royal style, showing cultural integration.

name: Statue of Senusret III
description: A striking statue of Senusret III known for its realistic facial features and powerful expression.

name: Hathor Column Capital from Dendera
description: A beautifully carved column capital depicting the face of the goddess Hathor, symbolizing joy and music.

name: Papyrus Column from Old Kingdom
description: A papyrus-shaped column symbolizing the marshlands of creation in ancient Egyptian cosmology.

name: Offering Relief of Ramses II
description: A carved relief showing Ramses II presenting offerings to the gods, symbolizing devotion and divine favor.

name: Mini Limestone Pyramidion
description: A small pyramidion that once crowned a pyramid, symbolizing the sun’s rays and rebirth.

name: Statue of Hapi (Nile God)
description: A statue of Hapi, the Nile god, shown carrying offerings symbolizing fertility and abundance.

name: Lintel of Ramses III
description: A granite lintel from the reign of Ramses III decorated with inscriptions and royal titles.

name: Statue of Sekhmet
description: A powerful statue of the lion-headed goddess Sekhmet, symbolizing war and healing.

name: Statue of Ptah
description: A statue of Ptah, the creator god, depicted in mummiform shape holding a staff of power.

name: Statue of Min
description: A statue of the fertility god Min, associated with regeneration and agricultural prosperity.

name: Column from Medinet Habu
description: A decorated column from Medinet Habu, featuring reliefs of Ramses III and religious scenes.

name: Head of Goddess Hathor
description: A finely carved head of the goddess Hathor, symbolizing love, music, and motherhood.

name: Statue of Anubis
description: A statue of Anubis, the jackal-headed god of mummification and the afterlife.

name: Small Obelisk of Ramses II
description: A smaller obelisk dedicated to Ramses II, inscribed with royal cartouches and solar symbols.

name: Horemheb before Amun
description: A statue of King Horemheb standing before the god Amun, reinforcing his divine support.

name: Seti I with Isis
description: A statue of Seti I accompanied by the goddess Isis, symbolizing protection and divine blessing.

name: Ramses II as a Child with Hauron
description: A statue showing Ramses II depicted as a child under the protection of the god Hauron.

name: Osiris Enthroned Statue
description: A seated statue of Osiris, god of the afterlife, symbolizing resurrection and eternal life.

name: Isis Nursing Horus
description: A tender depiction of Isis nursing the infant Horus, symbolizing motherhood and divine protection.

name: Statue of Queen Nefertari
description: A refined statue of Queen Nefertari, the Great Royal Wife of Ramses II.

name: Sarcophagus of Queen Hetepheres
description: An Old Kingdom sarcophagus belonging to Queen Hetepheres, mother of Khufu.

name: Granite Sarcophagus of Merneptah
description: A massive granite sarcophagus belonging to King Merneptah, richly inscribed.

name: Quartzite Sarcophagus of Thutmose I
description: A quartzite sarcophagus belonging to Thutmose I, richly decorated with protective spells.

name: Sarcophagus of Tiye
description: Wooden coffin of Queen Tiye, adorned with gilded funerary inscriptions and divine motifs.

name: Granite Sarcophagus from Dynasty 26
description: A massive granite sarcophagus from the 26th Dynasty, designed to protect the deceased's body for eternity.

name: Coffin of Hatshepsut
description: The original coffin of Queen Hatshepsut before alterations, showcasing exquisite New Kingdom artistry.

name: Painted Wooden Coffin Lid
description: A painted lid of a wooden coffin from the Deir el-Bahari cache, decorated with colorful motifs.

name: Sarcophagus of Psamtik
description: A limestone sarcophagus belonging to Psamtik, adorned with hieroglyphs and divine imagery.

name: Sarcophagus with Book of the Dead Texts
description: A limestone sarcophagus featuring hieroglyphs from the Book of the Dead to guide the deceased.

name: Anthropoid Sarcophagus
description: A human-shaped (Anthropoid) granite sarcophagus from the Late Period.

name: Sarcophagus with Funerary Texts
description: A coffin bearing sacred texts from the Book of the Dead for protection in the afterlife.

name: Tutankhamun's Golden Mask
description: The iconic funerary mask of solid gold and semi-precious stones, representing the boy king as a god.

name: Inner Golden Coffin
description: The innermost coffin of Tutankhamun made of solid gold, found inside the middle coffin.

name: Outer Quartzite Sarcophagus
description: The massive stone box that housed the golden coffins, decorated with protective deities at the corners.

name: Gilded Shrines (Four Shrines)
description: Four nested gilded wooden shrines that surrounded the king's sarcophagus in the burial chamber.

name: Royal Golden Throne
description: A wooden throne covered in gold, featuring a scene of the king and his wife Ankhesenamun.

name: Great War Chariot
description: One of the large military-style chariots used for ceremonies or hunts.

name: Ceremonial Chariot
description: A highly decorated chariot designed for royal processions.

name: The Ka Statue
description: Life-sized black wooden statue representing the king's spirit, acting as a tomb guardian.

name: Alabaster Canopic Chest
description: A chest used to store the four jars containing the king's internal organs.

name: Statues of the Protective Goddesses
description: Four golden statues of Isis, Nephthys, Selket, and Neith guarding the canopic chest.

name: Cow-Headed Funerary Bed
description: A ritual bed representing the celestial cow Mehet-Weret, carrying the king to the sky.

name: Painted Wardrobe Chest
description: A famous chest decorated with detailed scenes of the king in battle against enemies.

name: Ostrich Feather Fan
description: A ceremonial fan with a golden handle, once held real ostrich feathers for the king's comfort.

name: Meteoric Iron Dagger
description: A rare dagger found on the king's mummy, made from iron of extraterrestrial (meteoric) origin.

name: Winged Scarab Necklace
description: A magnificent piece of jewelry featuring a central scarab made of Libyan desert glass.

name: Anubis Shrine
description: A wooden statue of Anubis in jackal form, reclining on a gilded shrine, used to protect the Treasury room.

name: King Hunting with Harpoon
description: A gilded statue of Tutankhamun standing on a papyrus skiff, throwing a harpoon.

name: Funerary Boat Models
description: A collection of 35 miniature wooden boats intended to transport the king's spirit across the celestial waters.

name: Ceremonial Hunting Shield
description: A shield decorated with a scene of the king as a sphinx trampling his enemies.

name: Royal Bow and Arrows
description: The king's composite bow and a set of arrows, representing his role as a warrior and hunter.

name: Golden Sandals
description: Exquisite gold sandals found on the feet of the king's mummy, mimicking woven reed sandals.

name: Senet Game Board
description: An ivory and ebony board for the game of Senet, symbolizing the passage of the soul to the afterlife.

name: Silver Trumpet
description: A rare ceremonial silver trumpet used in rituals, known for its clear, piercing sound.

name: Bronze Trumpet
description: The copper/bronze counterpart to the silver trumpet, used in military or ritual contexts.

name: Childhood Ebony Chair
description: A small, beautifully crafted chair made of ebony and inlaid with ivory, used by the king as a child.

name: Blue Glass Headrest
description: A ceremonial headrest made of deep blue glass, representing the horizon (Akhet).

name: King's Ushabti Figurine
description: A small wooden statue of the king, intended to perform tasks for him in the afterlife.

name: Alabaster Perfume Vessel
description: An intricately carved alabaster container used for holding precious oils and perfumes.

name: Wooden Clothing Bust (Mannequin)
description: A lifelike wooden bust of the king, likely used as a mannequin for his robes and jewelry.

name: Scarab Signet Ring
description: A gold signet ring featuring a finely carved lapis lazuli scarab with the king's cartouche.

name: Inscribed Ivory Box
description: A delicately carved ivory box with intricate inscriptions and scenes from the king's life.

name: Gilded Statue of Ptah
description: A divine statue of the creator god Ptah, covered in gold leaf, found among the ritual objects.

name: Khufu's Solar Boat
description: The oldest intact wooden vessel in the world, used for the Pharaoh's funerary journey.

name: Small Statue of Khufu
description: The only known three-dimensional representation of Khufu, a tiny ivory masterpiece.

name: Hetepheres Collection (Bed/Chair)
description: Domestic royal furniture from the Old Kingdom belonging to the mother of Khufu.

name: Egyptian Scribe (GEM Copy)
description: A representation of the administrative class, showing a seated scribe ready to write.

name: Victory Stele of Merneptah
description: A monumental stele recording the military triumphs of King Merneptah.

name: Amenemhat III (Lion Head)
description: A unique representation of the king with lion-like strength and features.

name: Fayum Portraits Selection
description: Lifelike funerary portraits from the Roman period showing a blend of cultures.

name: Nefertiti and Akhenaten Artworks
description: Artistic representations of Akhenaten and Nefertiti from the revolutionary Amarna period.
